sanskrit: काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं सन्न्यासं कवयो विदुः। सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः॥
transliteration: kāmyānāṃ karmaṇāṃ nyāsaṃ sannyāsaṃ kavayo viduḥ | sarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṃ prāhus tyāgaṃ vicakṣaṇāḥ ||
literal_meaning: The wise call the renunciation of desire-prompted actions 'sannyāsa'; the discerning call the abandonment of all fruits of action 'tyāga'.
contextual_meaning: True letting-go is not physical withdrawal but the inner surrender of expected rewards; maturity is measured by dropping entitlement to outcomes.
psychological_state: Post-achievement emptiness prompting re-evaluation of success definitions
trigger_scenario: After a promotion you fought for, the thrill fades in days and you wonder 'Is this all?'—realizing the prize didn't fill the hole.
anchor_text: Do your work, then let go of what it brings you.
